reasoning: GenDB Q3 runs at 938.2ms vs DuckDB target of 77.8ms (12.1x gap). The dominant bottleneck is the orders scan (702.7ms, 75% of runtime), which is single-threaded and doesn't leverage zone maps. Combining thread parallelism (14-17x potential speedup) with zone map skipping (1.75-2x) and Bloom filtering (1.6x join speedup) targets all three major bottlenecks simultaneously and can reduce execution time to ~80-120ms (8-12x speedup), competitive with DuckDB.
focus_areas: Parallelize orders scan across 64 cores using morsel-driven execution
Implement zone map skipping for o_orderdate filter to eliminate ~7.5M row I/O
Add Bloom filter for qualified orders to reduce hash table probes in lineitem scan
strategy_notes: Apply three high-impact, low-risk optimizations in parallel: (1) Load zone maps for orders.o_orderdate, skip blocks where block_max < 1995-03-15 (estimate 50% block elimination). (2) Parallelize orders scan loop with 64 threads, each building local hash map, then merge. (3) Build 2-hash Bloom filter after orders scan, use for fast rejection in lineitem scan. These target io_bound, cpu_bound, and join bottlenecks respectively. Expected combined effect: 8-12x speedup closing gap with DuckDB from 12.1x to <2x.
notes: All three optimizations are proven low-risk techniques. Zone map and parallelism directly address the 702.7ms bottleneck. Bloom filter reduces 163.1ms join phase. If successful, expect GenDB Q3 to reach 100-150ms range in iteration 1, enabling final iteration to focus on remaining gap (hash table optimization, SIMD refinement). Monitor for correctness regression during merge phase when combining local hash maps across 64 threads.
